question: Create an `Employee` class with `name` and `id`. Design a `Team` class that manages an array of `Employee` pointers to represent team members. Demonstrate adding employees to the team without the team owning their memory.

test_cases.expected:
--- Development Team (Attempting to display dangling pointers) ---
Employee: Alice (ID: 101)
Employee: Bob (ID: 102)
Employee: Charlie (ID: 103)

--- QA Team (Attempting to display dangling pointers) ---
Employee: David (ID: 104)

errors: The `Team::addEmployee` method takes an `Employee` object by value. A pointer to this temporary object is then stored in the `members` vector. When the temporary `Employee` object is destroyed at the end of `addEmployee`, the stored pointer becomes dangling, leading to undefined behavior upon subsequent access (e.g., in `displayTeam`).
